sharedStateObservations.observation: Auth middleware pattern: PUBLIC_PATHS entries use paths relative to where middleware is mounted. When mounted at '/api', auth routes (e.g., /api/auth/login) appear as /auth/login in req.path
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/middleware/auth.ts - PUBLIC_PATHS uses '/auth/login' etc. The comment explicitly notes: 'When mounted at /api, the auth routes appear as /auth/*'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Profile route is mounted before auth middleware applies - profileRoutes bypasses auth middleware when POST /api/profile is called, since /profile is in PUBLIC_PATHS
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.ts - profileRoutes mounted at '/api/profile' and middleware mounted at '/api'. req.path will be '/profile' for POST /api/profile

summary: Fix is correct and complete. Added '/profile' to PUBLIC_PATHS in auth middleware (commit 59adb57). This allows POST /api/profile to be called without authentication, enabling profile creation during onboarding. The fix is minimal and targeted - only the single line was changed. All validators pass (test, typecheck, lint).
